items.prompt: Which trio of challenges does this part emphasize for IoT collection?
choices: Crowdfunding, HIT pay, and gold standards only
items.explain: Section 9.3.5 frames scalability, security, and privacy as core IoT collection challenges.

items.prompt: End-to-end encryption and device authentication are recommended security practices for IoT pipelines.
items.explain: Section 9.3.6 lists encryption, authentication/authorization, and secure edge devices.

items.prompt: Example 9.20 (wearable health edge devices) stresses the need to:
choices: Secure devices and transmit health data safely to clinicians
Store passwords in plaintext on the wearable
Disable all encryption to save battery always
items.explain: Tamper resistance and secure transmission protect patient privacy in health edge sensing.

items.prompt: Name one ethical concern raised by large-scale IoT collection besides security breaches.
items.rubric: Accept data ownership disputes, data overload, or surveillance/privacy erosion.
items.sample_answer: Surveillance and privacy erosion when public or personal tracking devices monitor people continuously.

items.prompt: Example 9.21 describes smart cities collecting from multiple urban sources such as traffic, environment, waste, and transit.
items.explain: Smart-city IoT is multi-source by design across infrastructure and services.
